Awake.text3:
Am I dead? Or is this a dream?
I can't feel anything...
Awake.text4: What was that? It felt so familiar...
TheHost.text1:
Welcome to The Oblivium.
As its ruler, I formally greet you.
TheHost.text12:
Now, more importantly...
What do you think of the decor?
I did it myself!
TheHost.text2:
(a strange voice)
Oh, but by 'myself,' I mean...
well, isn't that something for
you to figure out? Haha!
TheHost.text3:
Haha! You'll understand in time.
For now, shouldn't you be asking
something more important?
Awake.text10: (Who am I...? Why can't I remember anything?)
Awake.text11:
Aiden... My name is Aiden,
I think...
TheHost.text4:
(a strange voice)
Haha! The kid's funny, trying to remember!
TheHost.text5:
Let me give you a little advice, Aiden.
*Here, nothing moves... unless you truly try.*
TheHost.text6:
Help the people in the village.
Maybe your memories will return.
Awake.text12: The village? But there's nothing here...
TheHost.text8:
Oh, one more thing. 
*Be careful with your emotions. 
Things might get harder otherwise.*
Panel.text1:
Navigation Directions in this complex space.
- To The Healer: Always downward
- To Nowhere: Everything else
Panel.text2:
Welcome to The Healer’s lair—made possible by
your questionable decisions and poor life choices.
Panel.text3:
Navigation Directions in this complex space.
- To The Healer: Always downward
- To Almost Nowhere: Almost everything else
Panel.text4:
A park with a relaxing musical zone.
Step to play chords: Top-Cm, Right-Gm, Left-Dm
Healer.text1:
Ah, so you’re the newcomer...
Aiden. You’re later than I expected.
I’ve been waiting for you for many years.
Aiden.text1:
Waiting for me? But I don’t even know you...
And where am I, anyway?
This house just appeared out of nowhere!
Healer.text2:
You’re in the Oblivium,
a place that exists between
what was and what is yet to be.
Healer.text3:
Are you alright, child?
I’ve been calling you,
but you didn’t respond.
Aiden.text3:
You... I’ve seen you before...
Ahh, my head...
Healer.text4:
Migraines?
Let me take a look.
Healer.text5:
Music... It’s curious, isn’t it?
It can help find balance,
align body and mind.
Healer.text6:
You seem calmer now.
Tell me, Aiden, can you recall what day it is?
Healer.text8:
I see... Near-total amnesia.
Perhaps it’s not such a bad thing...
Healer.text9:
The Oblivium is a strange place...
And a generous one. It will allow
you to reclaim your memories,
one by one.
Healer.text10:
Here, everything changes based on your feelings.
But be careful. What you recover isn’t just 
an image of the past, it’s a reflection of 
yourself.
Healer.text11:
Yes, and sometimes that reflection can 
be hard to face.
Your mind, be it light or shadow, will
 guide you...or lead you astray.
Healer.text13:
Here, take this journal. It will help
you track your state of mind and the
memories you recover.
Healer.text14:
One day, you’ll have to make
a final choice. But for now,
it’s too soon to decide...
Healer.text15:
Keep the journal close.
One day, it will reveal what you seek.
ElderlyMan.text1: You startled me!\| I was lost in thought...
ElderlyMan.text2:
I tried to find my way to my son's village,
but the path keeps hiding from me.
ElderlyMan.text3:
I can see in your eyes 
the same look as my son...
\| Always searching, yet never truly finding.

Perhaps if you help me find my way,
it might guide you as well.
ElderlyMan.text4:
But I don’t know where to begin...
Maybe there’s something in my house
—or outside—that could help.
ElderlyMan.text5:
Maybe there’s something in my house
—or outside—that could help finding the path
to the village.
Elderly.text61:
This sadness...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been
years I didn't speak to him...
Elderly.text62:
This anger...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been
years I didn't speak to him...
Elderly.text63:
This fear...\| My son composed this music
after we had argued... It's been
years I didn't speak to him...
Elderly.text7:
Now I remember... the direction to the village! 
You must cross a long forest to reach the 
bridge!
Elderly.text8:
To get to the village, you must cross a long 
forest to reach the bridge!
Aiden.text11:
It's best not to venture out if
I don't know my way around.
Information.text1:
On the bedside table rests an old vinyl player.
Would you like to play a record?
Information.text2:
The chosen vinyl conveys sorrow, with chords:
 Cm-Gm-Dm-Dm-Gm-Cm.
Would you like to play it again?
Information.text3:
Composed in sorrow, by an unknown artist, 
with chords: Cm-Gm-Dm-Dm-Gm-Cm.
Information.text4:
The chosen vinyl conveys anger, with chords:
 Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Dm-Cm.
Would you like to play it again?
Information.text5:
Composed in anger, by an unknown artist, 
with chords: Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Dm-Cm.
Information.text6:
The chosen vinyl conveys fear, with chords:
 Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Cm-Dm.
Would you like to play it again?
Information.text7:
Composed in fear, by an unknown artist, 
with chords: Cm-Dm-Gm-Gm-Cm-Dm.
Information.text8:
You step on the musical square, but nothing plays.
It seems you lack the inspiration to create music.
Information.text9:
It is said that long ago, an old man walked so often 
toward the village that his steps became a path 
for all to follow.
Information.text11: Maybe I could go check out the park now...
Doubts1.text: Is this the right path? I see no end.
Doubts3.text:
If I turn back now,
will it be alright?
Doubts4.text: He said to go straight, they always say that.
Doubts42.text: “Keep going!” What if there’s nowhere to turn?
Doubts5.text:
No signs,
no guide,
just the weight of my thoughts.
Doubts6.text:
At what point does doubt
become insight?
Doubts9.text: Well boy, you should try harder.
Doubts10.text: Is he even worth watching? What a waste of time.
Information.text10: Cross the bridge to reach the village.
Aiden.text12:
Ugh, the bridge seems to be broken.
What should I do?\|
*I have a feeling this could affect
my mind or my memories.*
Choice.text2: Don’t do anything and go find the old man.
Aiden.text13:
Ugh, I can't seem to be able to repair
the bridge...I better go find the old man.
Elderly.text12:
You fixed this bridge alone?
Impressive! We’re almost there,
I can feel it.
Aiden.text14: I’d rather not go through the forest now.
Elderly.text9:
The village is just ahead.
Thank you, young one...
Elderly.text10:
I hope my son will agree to see me...
\|Family — such a strange bond, isn't it?
 The source of the greatest joys, and 
the deepest sorrows.
Elderly.text11:
Take this. I hope this token can 
help you find what you’re looking for.
Aiden.text15: A token?...Wait... That's a memory token!
grandma.text2:
Aiden, what are you doing daydreaming 
like that? Come on, I’ll push you on 
the swing!
grandma.text3:
*This child has already been through far 
too much for his age...*
littleaiden.text1: Look, Grandma! I'm touching the sky!
grandma.text4:
Careful, little bird!
Don’t fly too far from me!
grandma.text5: *I had to take him away from his own mother...*
grandma.text6:
Hm?
Ah... yes, you are, sweetheart.
cutscene1C.text: That little boy… is that me?
Grandma.text8: Look up, Aiden. The sky is really beautiful, isn’t it?
littleaiden.text4:
Wow! \|Do you see that Grandma...
The stars, they shine like little lamps!
Grandma.text9:
Yes, I see it. And look, if you trace lines
between the stars, you can draw in the sky.
littleaiden.text52:
Grandma, look! \|If I connect the one on the 
left with the one on the right and back to 
the left, it looks like a chicken!
Grandma.text10:
Yes, I see your chicken. Do you want us to
draw some friends for it?
littleaiden.text6:
Hmm... I’m not sure. It’s next to that 
lonely star over there that looks like Mom.
Do you think they can be friends?
Grandma.text11:
Yes, my heart, they can be the best friends in
the world.
littleaiden.text7: Do you think a star could fall into my pocket?
Grandma.text12:
Maybe so... But if a star falls into your 
pocket, you’ll have to take care of it
like a treasure.
littleaiden.text9:
Say Grandma, if the stars are treasures,
when people die do they join the stars?
Grandma.text13:
Oh, my heart... Maybe so. But we are all 
here with you, and for a very long 
time. So don’t you worry.
littleaiden.text10:
Alright...\|But if ever...\|I’ll look for 
her in the sky.
